zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins是John Hawkins的女兒，於1539年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇。她於1548年與John Bourchier Sayer在英國結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1595年在荷蘭努-布拉班特享年56歲，埋葬在荷蘭努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins是John Hawkins的女儿，于1539年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇。她于1548年与John Bourchier Sayer在英国结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1595年在荷兰努-布拉班特享年56岁，埋葬在荷兰努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。

nameMeanings.name: Hawkins
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Hawkin , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: habitational name, with excrescent -s, from Hawkinge (Kent). The placename derives from the Old English personal name Heafoc or Old English heafoc ‘hawk’ + the placename forming suffix -ing. This name has been assimilated to the patronymic surname in Devon from Sir John Hawkyns (1532–95), victor against the Spanish Armada (1588), who was a member of the Devon family of Hawkins, a branch of a Kentish family from the village of Hawkinge. They held land in Plymouth as long ago as 1480.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.description: William of Orange was recognized as viceroy of Holland, Friesland and Utrecht.
After the signing of the Treaty of Utrecht in 1579, Brabant became a regular battlefield between the Republic of the Netherlands (Protestant) and Spain (Catholic).

William of Orange (1533-1584), Prince of Orange (1538-1584), Count of Nassau (1559-1584), and first stadholder of the United Provinces of the Netherlands, was assassinated by Burgundian Balthasar Gerard (25) with a handgun. Philip II of Spain had called for a volunteer assassin due to William's reluctance take a public stand on religious issues. William was succeeded by his 17-year-old son, Maurice of Nassau.

historicEvents.title: The Golden Age
historicEvents.description: "The 17th century era, from roughly 1585 to 1672, was considered to be Amsterdam's ""Golden Age"". The city had formed a worldwide trading network with countries like Africa, Indonesia, Brazil, and North America. The city grew significantly during this time, and was regarded as the wealthiest city in the western world."

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins was born in 1539, in Holy Roman Empire as the daughter of John Hawkins. She married John Bourchier Sayer in 1548, in England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1595, in North Brabant, Netherlands, at the age of 56, and was buried in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins est née en 1539, à Saint-Empire romain, fille de John Hawkins. Elle a épousé John Bourchier Sayer en 1548, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1595, à Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Amsterdam, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins wurde 1539 in Heiliges Römisches Reich, als Tochter von John Hawkins geboren. Sie hat 1548 in England John Bourchier Sayer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1595 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Nord-Brabant, Niederlande und wurde in Amsterdam, Nord-Holland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins è nata il nel 1539 nel Heiliges Römisches Reich come figlia di John Hawkins. Lei sposato John Bourchier Sayer nel 1548, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1595, nel Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolta a Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkinsは1539年にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチでJohn Hawkinsの娘として生まれた。1548年にイギリスでJohn Bourchier Sayerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1595年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントで56歳で死去し，オランダノオルド-hオランドアムステルダムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins(은)는 1539년 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치에서 John Hawkins의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1548년 영국에서 John Bourchier Sayer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1595년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 아스터다에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins nasceu em 1539, em Heiliges Römisches Reich filha de John Hawkins. Ela casou-se com John Bourchier Sayer em 1548, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1595, em Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, com 56 anos, e foi sepultada em Amsterdam, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins родилась в 1539 г. в местности хилигесь рoмищесь раич. Её отца звали John Hawkins. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Bourchier Sayer в 1548 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1595 г. в местности нурд-брабант, Нидерланды в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности Амстердам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hawkins nació en 1539, en Heiliges Römisches Reich como hija de John Hawkins. Se casó con John Bourchier Sayer en 1548, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1595, en Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultada en Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos.
